sanskrit:
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः।
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम्॥
transliteration: kāma eṣa krodha eṣa rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ | mahāśano mahā-pāpmā viddhy enam iha vairiṇam ||
literal_meaning: This desire, this anger, born from the mode of passion, all-devouring, greatly sinful—know this as your enemy here.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the true antagonist: not external foes but the internal chain-reaction of craving → frustration → rage. It is a metabolic fire that first promises fulfillment, then consumes the host.
psychological_state: Arjuna confronts the projection of his own disowned aggression; the battlefield becomes a mirror for the shadow.
trigger_scenario: You scroll past an influencer’s post, feel a stab of craving, buy the gadget, still feel empty, snap at your partner—same neuro-chemical loop Krishna flags.
anchor_text: Spot craving before it turns to anger—pause the spark.
